# IAPKit ŌĆö full reference

> Receipt-validation SaaS managed by OpenIAP. https://kit.openiap.dev
> Source of truth for every fact here: the repo at
> github.com/hyodotdev/openiap-kit (private) and the in-dashboard docs at
> https://kit.openiap.dev/docs. If something below conflicts with live
> behavior, the live server wins ŌĆö this file is regenerated from the repo.

## Architecture

Single-package repo deployed as one Fly.io machine:

- `src/` ŌĆö React 19 SPA (dashboard, auth, usage, projects, API keys, docs)
- `server/` ŌĆö Hono + Bun server for `/api/v1/*` + `/v1/*` + SPA fallback
- `packages/mcp-server/` ŌĆö MCP server used by `/mcp`, ChatGPT connectors,
  Claude/Cursor/Codex, and self-hosted IAPKit assistant workflows
- `convex/` ŌĆö Convex functions (auth, orgs, projects, receipts)
- `public/` ŌĆö static assets (served by Vite in dev, Hono in prod)

The Convex backend is imported by both the frontend (via `@/convex` barrel
at `src/convex.ts`) and the Hono server (via `./convex` direct path).

## Authentication

Every API request must carry a Bearer token:

```http
Authorization: Bearer openiap-kit_<your-project-key>
```

Each project has its own API key. Keys are stored in Convex
(`convex/apiKeys`), looked up by SHA-256 hash, and can be rotated from the
dashboard. Dashboard users sign in via GitHub / Google OAuth or email-OTP
(Resend).

The MCP endpoint at `/mcp` uses the same IAPKit project key. MCP clients may
send it as `Authorization: Bearer <project-key>`. A self-hosted MCP server can
instead set `IAPKIT_API_KEY` so ChatGPT never receives the project key directly.
This is not an OpenAI API key.

## Endpoints

Mounted at both `/v1/*` (canonical) and `/api/v1/*` (alias).

### POST /v1/purchase/verify

Verify an in-app purchase. The body is a tagged union discriminated on
`store`. Response always has shape `{ isValid: boolean, state: <enum> }`.

Request (Apple):
```json
{ "store": "apple", "jws": "eyJhbGciOi..." }
```

Request (Google):
```json
{ "store": "google", "purchaseToken": "ljhjpg..." }
```

Request (Meta Horizon / Quest):
```json
{ "store": "horizon", "userId": "1234567890", "sku": "coin_pack_100" }
```

IAPKit does not expose the Meta App Secret to the client. The dashboard
stores it as a write-only credential and the server composes the
`OC|APP_ID|APP_SECRET` access token when calling
`POST https://graph.oculus.com/{APP_ID}/verify_entitlement`.

### Aliases

- `POST /v1/verify-purchase` ŌĆö identical handler, kept for the path
  documented in the original `api.iapkit.com` spec.
- `POST /api/v1/*` ŌĆö every `/v1/*` path is also reachable under `/api/v1/*`.

### GET /v1 / GET /v1/openapi

`/v1` serves a Redoc UI for the generated OpenAPI spec. `/v1/openapi`
returns the raw JSON. Both are unauthenticated.

### GET /health

```json
{ "ok": true }
```

Intentionally cheap ŌĆö no Convex round-trip. Used by Fly.io liveness /
readiness probes. Sentry is configured to drop `/health` transactions so
probe traffic doesn't dominate trace quota.

### POST /mcp

MCP Streamable HTTP endpoint for ChatGPT and other MCP clients. Exposes tools
with the `iapkit_` prefix: setup snippets, status checks, diagnostics, product
catalog reads/writes, subscriber views, sandbox guidance, webhook simulation,
and project inspection. Full setup guide:
`/docs/ai-assistants/chatgpt-plugin`.

## Harmonized purchase states

| State                     | `isValid` | Meaning                                              |
| ------------------------- | :-------: | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| `ENTITLED`                | true      | Paid, not refunded, entitlement active               |
| `PENDING_ACKNOWLEDGMENT`  | true      | Android: paid but awaiting acknowledgement           |
| `READY_TO_CONSUME`        | true      | iOS: consumable ready to be fulfilled                |
| `PENDING`                 | false     | In progress or awaiting confirmation                 |
| `CONSUMED`                | false     | Google Play: consumable already fulfilled            |
| `CANCELED`                | false     | Refunded, revoked, or canceled                       |
| `EXPIRED`                 | false     | Subscription past its expiry date                    |
| `INAUTHENTIC`             | false     | Receipt not recognized by the store                  |
| `UNKNOWN`                 | false     | State could not be determined                        |

## Status codes

| Code | Body                            | When                                              |
| ---- | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| 200  | `{ isValid, state }`            | Verification ran                                  |
| 400  | `INVALID_INPUT`                 | Malformed body, unknown store, oversized input    |
| 401  | `MISSING_API_KEY`               | No `Authorization` header                         |
| 403  | `INVALID_API_KEY`               | Wrong scheme, malformed, or unknown key           |
| 429  | `RATE_LIMITED`                  | Per-key bucket empty; honor `Retry-After`         |
| 500  | `UNKNOWN_ERROR`                 | Server-side failure; include `X-Correlation-Id`   |

Error body shape:
```json
{
  "errors": [
    {
      "code": "META_HORIZON_APP_SECRET_NOT_CONFIGURED",
      "message": "Meta Horizon App Secret is not set for this project.",
      "path": "horizon.appSecret"
    }
  ]
}
```

`path` is present on validation errors (400) and points at the offending
field. Store-specific failure codes carry details in `message` ŌĆö see the
per-store docs pages.

## Response headers

Every authenticated response (2xx, validation 4xx, 429) carries:

- `X-Correlation-Id` ŌĆö UUIDv4, matches the stdout log line
- `X-RateLimit-Limit` ŌĆö bucket capacity for this API key
- `X-RateLimit-Remaining` ŌĆö tokens left in the bucket

On 429 the response also carries `Retry-After` in seconds. 401 / 403
responses from the auth layer run before rate-limit middleware and do not
carry these headers.

## Rate limits

In-memory token bucket keyed on SHA-256(api-key). Defaults: 600-request
burst + 10 req/sec steady state (Ōēł 600 req/min sustained). Tunable on
self-hosted deployments via `RATE_LIMIT_CAPACITY` and
`RATE_LIMIT_REFILL_PER_SEC`. The Map is capped at 10,000 entries with LRU
eviction (`RATE_LIMIT_MAX_STORE`).

## Input size caps

- request body Ōēż 32 KB before JSON parsing
- `jws` Ōēż 16 KB (Apple)
- `purchaseToken` Ōēż 2 KB (Google)
- `userId` Ōēż 256 chars (Horizon)
- `sku` Ōēż 256 chars (Horizon)

Oversized fields return `400 INVALID_INPUT`; oversized request bodies return
`413 PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE`. Neither path calls the upstream store, so a
misbehaving client can't burn Apple / Google / Meta quota.

## Structured logs

Every verify request emits one JSON line to stdout:

```json
{
  "level": "info",
  "kind": "verify_request",
  "corrId": "6ebb9c9e-2e6e-4f9a-9bf2-4a6a9d5f9d20",
  "method": "POST",
  "path": "/v1/purchase/verify",
  "statusCode": 200,
  "durationMs": 187,
  "apiKeyHash": "c3d1f4a2b1e27f0a",
  "store": "google",
  "isValid": true,
  "state": "ENTITLED"
}
```

The API key is never logged in plaintext ŌĆö only the first 16 hex chars of
its SHA-256 prefix (the same value the rate limiter keys on).

## Outbound retries

Calls to Google Play's Android Publisher API and Meta Graph API are
wrapped in an exponential-backoff retry (max 3 attempts, base 200 ms, cap
2 s, full jitter). Retries fire on HTTP 5xx and Node network errors
(`ECONNRESET`, `ETIMEDOUT`, `EAI_AGAIN`, ŌĆ”). 4xx responses ŌĆö including 404
and 410, which are deterministic ŌĆö are **not** retried.

Apple calls (App Store Server API) follow the same policy.

## Graceful shutdown

The server installs `SIGTERM` and `SIGINT` handlers that call
`Bun.serve().stop()` and drain in-flight `/v1/*` and `/api/v1/*` requests
before the process exits. Fly.io sends `SIGTERM` before stopping a
machine, so rolling deploys don't cut off requests mid-verify.

## Sentry

Two Sentry projects ŌĆö one for the Bun server (`@sentry/bun`), one for the
browser SPA (`@sentry/react`). Default `tracesSampleRate` is 0.05; errors
are sampled at 1.0. `/health` transactions are dropped via
`ignoreTransactions` so Fly probes don't dominate the trace budget.

## Data model (Convex)

Domains under `convex/` follow a CQRS layout ŌĆö each has `query.ts`,
`mutation.ts`, `internal.ts`, and sometimes `action.ts`:

- `users/`, `userProfiles/` ŌĆö auth identities + profile settings
- `organizations/` ŌĆö top-level account boundary; one or more members
- `projects/` ŌĆö a single app; holds store credentials (Apple / Google /
  Horizon), owns API keys, owns verify events
- `apiKeys/` ŌĆö per-project bearer tokens, stored hashed
- `purchases/` ŌĆö receipt validation history (apple, google, horizon)
- `certificates/` ŌĆö uploaded `.p8` / service-account JSON blobs
- `files/` ŌĆö generic file store used by certificates
- `migrations/` ŌĆö one-off schema migrations

`convex/schema.ts` is the canonical data model. Never edit
`convex/_generated/` by hand ŌĆö it's regenerated by the Convex CLI.

## Deployment

- Image: `Dockerfile` produces a single-stage binary image; multi-stage
  build compiles the SPA (`vite build`), compiles the server
  (`bun build --compile`), and copies both into the final image.
- Host: Fly.io app `openiap-kit` (one machine, sufficient for current
  load).
- CI: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs typecheck + build + server smoke on
  every PR. `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` runs `flyctl deploy` on merge
  to `main`.
